cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question says today is the second day of the third month of 1966, so that would be March 2nd, 1966, right? Because the third month is March, and the second day would be the 2nd. So if today is March 2nd, then tomorrow would be March 3rd. Let me check the options. Looking at the options, (A) is 03/03/1966, which would be March 3rd. But wait, the question asks for the date tomorrow. If today is March 2nd, then adding one day would make it March 3rd. So that would be option A. But let me make sure there's no trick here. For example, is there any possibility that the date format is different? The question specifies MM/DD/YYYY, so month first, then day. So 03 for March, 03 for the 3rd, and 1966. So option A would be correct. Let me check the other options just to be thorough. Option B is 03/24, which is way later in the month. Option C is 03/04, which is the 4th. Option D is February 28th, which would be the last day
